bullets.text: March CPI rose 0.9% m/m (3.3% y/y); gasoline/energy surge tied to Iran conflict, complicating disinflation and Fed policy.
Real average hourly earnings fell 0.6% in March, showing inflation outpaced wages and squeezing consumer purchasing power.
Higher gasoline prices push up early estimates for 2027 Social Security COLA, implying larger federal outlays and benefit adjustments.
U.S.-Iran negotiations hinge on Lebanon ceasefire and Iranian asset releases; Hormuz security risks (mines, shipping) keep oil and jet-fuel supply fragile.
Europe faces potential jet-fuel crunch within weeks and fuel-price protests (e.g., Ireland), highlighting war-driven energy shocks’ spillovers to transport and politics.
Federal Reserve approved Burke & Herbert Financial Services’ application, signaling continued regulatory clearance for bank expansion/structural changes.
Xi warned of ‘Taiwan independence’ threats in first cross-strait opposition talks in a decade, underscoring persistent geopolitical risk for Asia supply chains.
Anthropic’s ‘Mythos’ AI prompted Powell/Bessent briefings with major banks on cyber/operational risk, signaling regulators’ growing focus on frontier-model threats.
TSMC reported record revenue up 35% y/y, reflecting sustained AI chip demand and reinforcing strength in advanced foundry capacity utilization.
CoreWeave shares jumped on a deal to power Anthropic’s Claude, highlighting surging demand for GPU cloud capacity and AI infrastructure monetization.
France plans to replace Windows with Linux to reduce dependence on U.S. tech vendors, accelerating ‘digital sovereignty’ procurement across governments.
New stool-based screening test reportedly detects ~90% of colorectal cancers, potentially expanding early detection and reducing reliance on invasive colonoscopy.
Researchers argue Alzheimer’s therapies may target the wrong mechanisms, suggesting a paradigm shift that could redirect drug development after repeated trial failures.
